the_signal.headline: AI governance becomes table-stakes for enterprise revenue systems
the_signal.body: n8n's new enterprise governance guide signals what's actually shipping now: compliance tooling isn't optional anymore for GTM teams deploying multi-agent systems at scale. If you're building agent-driven revenue workflows, governance architecture determines whether you can sell into enterprises—not whether it's nice to have.

tool_drop.name: API to MCP
tool_drop.what: Converts REST APIs into Model Context Protocol compatible interfaces for direct LLM integration.
tool_drop.why: Eliminates the routing layer friction between your revenue data APIs and agent decision-making—agents get native data access without custom integrations.

thread_of_day.title: Integration Patterns: How To Choose for Your Architecture
thread_of_day.takeaway: The pattern you pick (sync vs async vs event-driven) determines whether your multi-agent system scales or collapses under state management debt—most GTM teams pick wrong on day one.

quote.text: Your first AI agent worked great. Then you added three more and the system became impossible to debug.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns

whats_moving.context: n8n's detailed governance comparison signals that sales teams now need compliance proofs before enterprise deals close—visibility into model decisions and audit trails are closing friction.
n8n's expanded orchestration framework guide shows the shift from workflow automation as a backend tool to orchestration as a required GTM capability for agent deployment.
New guidance on sync/async/event-driven patterns suggests most teams are hitting scaling walls because they don't match their agent communication model to their data architecture.

act_on_this: Audit your current multi-agent system's integration pattern against n8n's framework—map whether you're using sync blocking calls when you should be async, or event-driven when you need consistency guarantees. Most revenue system failures aren't LLM problems; they're integration topology problems.

numbers.stat: n8n targeting 200-person UK team by 2029 (UK team already tripled YoY)
numbers.context: Enterprise orchestration platforms are capital-intensive and betting hard on GTM/compliance as their moat—the market is consolidating around platforms that handle governance natively rather than bolt-on tooling.
